description.en-US: A short consultation on the sacramental formula of absolution — whether it should be indicative ("I absolve you") or deprecative ("May God absolve you"). Latin only.
description.la: Responsum breve de forma sacramentali absolutionis — utrum indicativa ("Ego te absolvo") an deprecativa ("Deus te absolvat") esse debeat.

sources.description: Latin text from the Leonine / Marietti editions (public domain), mirrored from aquinas.cc.

title.en-US: C. 1 - That it is unwise to say that '*I absolve you'* is not the form of absolution
title.la: C. 1 - That it is unwise to say that '*I absolve you'* is not the form of absolution

title.en-US: C. 2 - The arguments of those who wish to prove that the given form of absolution should not be maintained, and their solutions
title.la: C. 2 - The arguments of those who wish to prove that the given form of absolution should not be maintained, and their solutions

title.en-US: C. 3 - With what frivolous arguments he attacks another solution to the problem, if this solution happens to be held
title.la: C. 3 - With what frivolous arguments he attacks another solution to the problem, if this solution happens to be held

title.en-US: C. 4 - That the imposition of the hand of the priest is not a necessary part of this sacrament
title.la: C. 4 - That the imposition of the hand of the priest is not a necessary part of this sacrament

title.en-US: C. 5 - Some frivolous arguments on the same topic and their solutions
title.la: C. 5 - Some frivolous arguments on the same topic and their solutions
